claimText: Uranium enrichment is the process of increasing the proportion of the uranium-235 isotope while removing uranium-238, primarily using centrifuges.
evidenceExcerpt: Uranium enrichment increases the proportion of U-235 while removing U-238, primarily using centrifuges.
examUse: Statement-based question on the definition and method of uranium enrichment.
distractorHooks: Swapping U-235 and U-238 (e.g., increasing U-238)
Mentioning incorrect methods like chemical distillation instead of centrifuges

claimText: Civilian nuclear power reactors typically require low-enriched uranium, containing 3 to 5 percent of the uranium-235 isotope.
evidenceExcerpt: Civilian nuclear power reactors typically use uranium enriched to 3-5% U-235.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question matching enrichment levels to their applications.
distractorHooks: Stating civilian reactors use greater than 20 percent enriched uranium
Claiming civilian reactors use natural uranium without any enrichment

claimText: Highly enriched uranium (HEU) contains more than 20 percent uranium-235, while weapons-grade uranium is enriched to approximately 90 percent uranium-235.
evidenceExcerpt: Highly enriched uranium (HEU) contains greater than 20% U-235 and has potential for weapons. Weapons-grade uranium is enriched to approximately 90% U-235.
examUse: Comparison or statement-based question on the thresholds for HEU and weapons-grade uranium.
distractorHooks: Confusing the 90 percent threshold with U-238 instead of U-235
